title: fix(sdk): relax video duration validation for new models
author: Circuit-Overtime

gist.headline: Director's cut unlocked
gist.blurb: We've relaxed the video duration limits in the SDK so you can play with all the shiny new models like Wan, LTX-2, and Grok Video Pro. The server handles the specifics, you just bring the popcorn.
gist.summary: Relaxed strict client-side video duration validation in the SDK to unblock generation for newer models like Wan, LTX-2, and Grok Video Pro.
gist.impact: Developers using the SDK can now generate videos up to 30 seconds long and access the latest video models without client-side errors.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a movie director. A clapperboard, film reels, maybe a cute robot holding a camera. The bee has a little director's beret or megaphone.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's beret, sitting on a stack of film reels next to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holding a clapperboard. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's beret, sitting on a stack of film reels next to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holding a clapperboard.
